title: Restorative Justice As A Procedural Instrument: A Review Of Procedural Law On Handling Drug Abusers
author: Anandy Satrio Purnomo,
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Drug abuse in Indonesia has become a complex problem that not only threatens public health but also burdens the criminal justice system through overcriminalization practices that lead to overcrowding in correctional institutions. This article analyzes the legal construction of restorative justice as a procedural instrument in handling drug abusers and identifies obstacles in its implementation. This research uses a normative juridical method with a statutory, conceptual, and case-based approach, analyzing regulations such as Law Number 35 of 2009 concerning Narcotics, Police Regulation Number 8 of 2021, Prosecutor's Regulation Number 15 of 2020, and Supreme Court Regulation Number 4 of 2023. The results show that Indonesia already has a normative restorative justice framework implemented through procedural mechanisms at the investigation stage (police discretion and conditional termination of investigation), the prosecution stage (termination of prosecution based on the principle of opportunity), and the trial stage (diversion and rehabilitation decisions). However, implementation faces obstacles in the form of ambiguous "self-serving" norms, inter-institutional authority conflicts, inconsistent assessment implementation, disparity in decisions, and limited rehabilitation facilities. This study recommends the need to harmonize restorative justice regulations within the Criminal Procedure Code (KUHAP), standardize assessment and rehabilitation mechanisms, increase facility capacity, and strengthen inter-institutional coordination within an integrated criminal justice system oriented toward the recovery and social reintegration of drug abusers.

keywords: Restorative Justice, Criminal Procedure, Drug Abusers, Rehabilitation, Criminal Justice System
